methods.name: Peer-Median

data.narrative:
Cartesia is a Series B AI & Software company that has raised $186M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 7 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private peers spanning Series A through Series C, with valuations ranging from $3.2B to $39.0B. The comp set includes Figure AI ($39.0B, Series C), Reflection AI ($25.0B, Series A), Applied Intuition ($15.0B, Series A), Skild AI ($14.0B, Series C), and Together AI ($8.3B, Series A), alongside one public comparable (Palantir).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression, using the $186M total raised as a proxy valuation and applying a typical 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, yields $353M. Peer-median analysis across the 7-company comp set produces $14.0B, reflecting the broad distribution of valuations in frontier AI at this stage. The two methods diverge significantly, reflecting the wide valuation dispersion in early-stage AI companies and the sensitivity of forward-multiple methodologies.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $300M to $16.1B, with a median of $7.2B. The wide range reflects genuine uncertainty in comparable selection and stage-appropriate multiples for frontier-tech software at Series B. Key uncertainties include whether Cartesia's capital efficiency and burn profile align with lower-stage comps or justify mid-stage pricing, and the applicability of the broader frontier AI comp universe to this specific technology and go-to-market trajectory.
